snippet: Typescript gets new Compiler and React's new Framework and GIthub's "Eternal September" ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌
body_text:
----------
A code review tool that I use personally and can recommend it highly. They also have a Slack bot that answer questions using older conversations. Extremely efficient. Give it a try

### **AI code review that knows your system the way you do.**

[Unblocked](https://getunblocked.com/code-review/?utm_source=helloworld&utm_medium=email&utm_campaign=codereview&utm_content=260221_primary) is the only AI code review tool that draws on context from your codebase, Slack, Jira, docs, PR history, and more. Get high-signal comments based on how your system actually works.

Try Free today (https://getunblocked.com/code-review/?utm_source=helloworld&utm_medium=email&utm_campaign=codereview&utm_content=260221_primary)


----------———————————————————————————

**Typescript gets a new Compiler!** **- **TypeScript 6.0 beta has arrived as the last version using its current JavaScript codebase and lays groundwork for the future Go-based compiler in TypeScript 7.0. It adjusts key compiler defaults, removes legacy options, and helps developers prepare for the upcoming architectural shift. The eventual Go rewrite aims to boost compile performance and reduce memory usage in large codebases.[ Read Blog Post.](https://devblogs.microsoft.com/typescript/announcing-typescript-6-0-beta/)

**Google Unveils Developer Knowledge API and MCP Server - **Google has released Android** **17 Beta 1 and is changing how developers get early access to the latest OS features by introducing** a continuous “always-on” Canary update channel **instead of the traditional spaced-out developer preview builds. This Canary channel delivers new features and APIs as soon as they pass internal testing, letting devs test and iterate faster with over-the-air updates with full platform stability targeted around March and a final release expected in mid-2026. [Read more.](https://www.developer-tech.com/news/android-17-beta-continuous-canary-updates-for-developers/)

[Full Stack Developer - @SGS](https://hw.glich.co/jobboard/jobs/full-stack-developer-at-sgs-dmq8yb)


----------———————————————————————————

## ChatGPT’s Deep Research Gets an Interactive Upgrade

OpenAI has rolled out a major overhaul to Deep Research in ChatGPT, transforming it from a passive “run it and wait” feature into a guided, interactive research experience. Instead of submitting a prompt and waiting for a finished report, users can now steer the process in real time constraining sources, refining direction mid-run, and shaping the scope as insights emerge. The result feels less like querying a chatbot and more like collaborating with a research assistant.

One of the most impactful upgrades is the ability to limit research to specific websites. This solves a long-standing issue with AI-assisted research: overbreadth. Analysts, journalists, and founders often know which domains they trust. By restricting the model to those sources, outputs become tighter, more relevant, and easier to verify. The system also supports connectors, allowing users to incorporate context from tools like email, documents, or calendars information that typically sits outside the model’s reach. For professionals working within established workflows, this makes Deep Research far more practical.

## Designed for Iterative, Real-World Work

Another critical improvement is the ability to intervene during a research run. Previously, if the model surfaced a promising but slightly misaligned direction, users had to restart from scratch. Now, they can interrupt the session, add constraints, redirect the focus, or clarify requirements on the fly. This mirrors how real research unfolds, iterative, exploratory, and responsive to new information.

The presentation layer has also been enhanced. Reports now open in a dedicated full-screen view optimized for reviewing long, citation-heavy outputs. Skimming sections, checking references, and navigating structured findings is less cramped and more fluid. For recurring, source-based tasks such as competitive analysis, market research, policy reviews, or investigative summaries this structured readability matters.

## Powered by GPT-5.2 and Agent-Style Workflows

Behind the scenes, Deep Research has moved to GPT-5.2, aligning it with OpenAI’s broader shift toward agent-like systems. Rather than treating ChatGPT as a single-shot answer engine, OpenAI is positioning it as a workflow partner capable of browsing, synthesizing, and integrating external tools. This reflects a clear product direction: AI that works across steps, not just responses.

There is also growing anticipation around GPT-5.3, especially following the release of GPT-5.3-Codex for coding workflows. Whether a general ChatGPT-facing GPT-5.3 will soon replace GPT-5.2 in Deep Research remains uncertain, but the trajectory suggests rapid iteration.

## A “Skills” Layer on the Horizon?

Alongside these research upgrades, signals point to the potential introduction of a first-party “Skills” layer in ChatGPT. Skills would function as installable, editable workflow instructions essentially packaging repeatable processes, constraints, and output expectations into reusable modules. Instead of reinventing the approach for each task, users could activate a predefined playbook.

If implemented, this would bring agent-style customization directly into ChatGPT without requiring a full custom agent stack. Teams co

Using Split Views to get more done!

https://thebrowser.company/
February 14, 2026
Sail through work (or play!) with Split Views
https://thebrowser.company/

Introducing Split Views…

…an easy way to put two sites side by side — or even top and bottom! — so you can get more done.

Keep scrolling to learn how to use it.
https://thebrowser.company/
✂️ Create a Split!
Just drag a tab from onto another tab in your Sidebar to create a Split!
https://thebrowser.company/
🔥 Hot Tip!
Hello! Nash (https://twitter.com/lafillemouen) here on Storytelling. I love keeping my screen feeling as large and clear as possible — so I always keep my sidebar hidden using CTRL S!
💬 Questions?
Check out Ellis’ video (https://www.youtube.com/watch?v=GwvHZgF_fWY) on multitasking in Arc, or just click Help > Contact the Team!

More soon!

snippet: 'Wuthering Heights' and more gothic movie picks, plus an exploration of erotic thrillers, and iconic on-screen romances. banner image Tragic Hearts: A Gothic Primer Tragic Hearts: A Gothic
body_text:
Tragic Hearts: A Gothic Primer
If 'Wuthering Heights' has got you in a dark and brooding mood, then we have just what you need. Here are our picks for cinema's must-watch gothic tales.

snippet: Hey, when was the last time you checked for new fresher openings? The current hiring window is about to close, and many grads are already securing interviews and moving ahead in the process. Browse
body_text:
GSIF 2025

Hey, when was the last time you checked for new fresher openings?

The current hiring window is about to close, and many grads are already securing interviews and moving ahead in the process.

Browse curated entry-level roles, apply in just a few clicks, and get directly in front of recruiters who are actively reviewing profiles. Once this phase wraps up, several of these opportunities may no longer be available.

Here’s what's in there:

1. Salaries up to ₹15 LPA 
2. 30,000+ fresher jobs across roles you actually want
3. Opportunities across 3,000+ companies like Paytm, PwC, EY, Deloitte, Blinkit & Accenture, all in one place, all for free

You should not let this opportunity slip from your hand

Ready to get hired? Click here or on the button below to send in your application today.

Apply Now

Ananth,

Today, I’m pleased to welcome back our guest expert, Arthur Mello, a senior data scientist who builds real-world ML systems (Python + SQL) and shares what he learns across LinkedIn (https://learn.educative.io/e3t/Ctc/RG+113/cz5H404/VWv-j54xn2XdW483-p_8tS691W8QwHkG5Kx_1HN6TZ7t43prCCW8wLKSR6lZ3kvW4RC46D22MnHhW3NtLQL11p8TqW7mYvj12RqYcLW8vjmKY1ccsdzVBH95K7yR8LxW6fscyy58KpWTVmpTQr8T9WtnN1WjLJzCF0bSVWgktx389h5qW4yzQbZ1qgG-FW2gllQz4SS4qfW7yRjyl2qWbq2W6YZ5lG80rhJCW5b5XXK2rY0nsW1RSk4k8twKfcW3F8dj-6whxnpW4B39FS1n2l_RW8Yh8dV7GRzysW3C6FyN6WxMtNW2p1Cpy7299m3W5Hv4Dd64lfCsW6n2QZz4Vgxh8W7YzbhX1HW5HRW3lgCDR6DXRSNW50ZzwM302FfzW224ryR5S_TCNW5T3Y0d4GW7h-W8FK4Pj2SzkN-f96GTl-04 ) , Medium (https://learn.educative.io/e3t/Ctc/RG+113/cz5H404/VWv-j54xn2XdW483-p_8tS691W8QwHkG5Kx_1HN6TZ7sv3prCCW7lCdLW6lZ3nPW6Dm3Gf64-8dlVm-Grw1BRKKcW64yHbS72MlF2W8fq0_H5mk4S1W53Pmv254lK6BN2BJrN_Tsym4W7yFRJ-2dVkwmW32HFbq9gTGYLW2rpCkx92MbfkW8WX1m96B0myRW3HcK8W6bftdYW3md7X-3qFgC6W6tXFGf1gFYJyW79MpRc10dYzWW1ftl2c6Tw7NTN1wz8zxTH9yWW24J-_x199ZF6W3GZwp84NtQmRVx89Tr8MX0hjN3HzMtLF6lh5W6TZk52235SVpW7jS6mf1n9wSNW8vY8yn1Gj1b4M5Glc6FhLNff2mq3l804 ) , and YouTube (https://learn.educative.io/e3t/Ctc/RG+113/cz5H404/VWv-j54xn2XdW483-p_8tS691W8QwHkG5Kx_1HN6TZ7sP3prCCW7Y8-PT6lZ3n5W4nLyr838c82hW718Jp25G4p8SW7zBNZl2QXs2lW2RfW_s85dgFSW1GnKZT58jpwMW6b2Z7m7s8HbsN695Frrgy5TRMZfv4BHkZf7W43_Gjr6HZ3xyW8cczTR21j5c6W6bLBxj3S7tmwVqnhSj67Y-jRW38hYXn1lqLmpN40DRRK5yrHDW18HfrT6HH906W2951-Z2FRt5NW4glTV11SxPdyW3PhzWC5MJzHwW4bRpZ144FfS5N8xCLB73Cq4mW33tsry84Hm2wW7N0KH-1sSbMtVV72xh6YwjSPN9hV4c7g1vCjW2ycXxy5GH1TdVrcbjy6Y0mH8f4BqDdn04 ) .

In this issue, Arthur shares a deep dive into how he built ZapDoc—a PDF-to-Excel application powered by FastAPI and Gemini (Vertex AI), including the end-to-end document processing pipeline, validation and reliability layers, and the core architectural decisions behind the product.

As a quick reminder, to mark a major milestone—3 million developers learning on Educative—we’re giving you 50% off (https://learn.educative.io/e3t/Ctc/RG+113/cz5H404/VWv-j54xn2XdW483-p_8tS691W8QwHkG5Kx_1HN6TZ7t43prCCW8wLKSR6lZ3m5W2GvQJk1Q55mZVtW61m9fyCkmVznW-58wbvCfW7szDZf23PGZ7N4N3G1jQ2D40W6Df7W_7xqgkbW72QZSk8xM9PRW7qFk4W589RD5W6260wm21cCZwW14CJTC8NvBFRW6SjJrq28NrbPW4kB8pY708wxNW76V1Zf1XGRzNW279Rdh5X5CGLW8XjPMB8Q3NcTW59-82k2M71YvW8zS6LF5bsLRcN2Fgp6sJrs0cVK8mK-8zCzdjW98Wtsf5jQJjjW5wP-F73ncH5bW2qP45h7Z2TZqW8Qbk144lmyY-W1PCzl_4TS_DHVT_qCx1QpfzfW49VnJj8kNZ-cM9YGP6WQm3-VGVyYc4D35pMf6P1hmP04 ) this month as a thank-you for being part of the journey.

Now, over to Arthur.

Hola everyone, Arthur here 🫡

By day, I’m a senior data scientist solving marketing problems with machine learning, SQL, and Python, because guessing isn’t a strategy (even if it sometimes feels like one).

Outside of work, I build and share practical projects across social media, mostly focused on what it takes to move from “cool demo” to something that actually holds up in production.

Today, I will break down how I built ZapDoc, a PDF-to-Excel application.

If you’re building AI-powered document workflows, I hope this provides a practical blueprint. Let’s get into it.

The origin story

ZapDoc is an AI-powered document processing platform that extracts structured data from PDF documents and delivers results in Excel format via email.

I didn’t start out wanting to build a document automation platform.

What I wanted was leverage. I’d seen firsthand how time-consuming and painful it was to pull structured data out of messy PDFs: invoices, CVs, contracts, proposals… all needing to be manually parsed, copy-pasted, or hand-entered into Excel.

So I asked myself: Could I build something that turns these documents into clean, structured spreadsheets, with zero effort from the user?

After a few weekends and sleepless nights, the answer became ZapDoc, a web tool that:

- Takes multiple PDFs or a .zip archive,
- Let's you define which fields you want to extract (e.g. “name”, “email”, “amount”, “date”),
- And returns a clean Excel file by email.

It sounds simple, but the devil is in the details.

From MVP to modular system

I built the first prototype over a weekend: FastAPI for the backend, hosted on Railway; a minimal React frontend on Vercel; and OpenAI for field extraction.

v0: prove it works

The early version was rough, but functional.

The idea was to make it free, so people would actually use it and I'd get some feedback. It had to be friction-free.

I tested it with real CVs and invoices. I posted in indie Discords to get feedback, asking people what they’d actually use it for.

One thing became clear: I didn’t need to support every document type. I needed to be amazing at just a few — like resumes, invoices, and RFPs.

Iterating

v1:

- .zip upload support
- Field templates (e.g., pre-fill “name” and “email” for resumes)
- Date formatting, better validation

v2:

- Usage analytics (without storing docs)
- Email capture → no sign up yet, but I you'd still need to give your e-mail address
- Templates for RFPs and proposals

v3:

- Auth system + Supabase-backed user DB
- Credit management service
- Payment flow & webhook integration
- A clean

snippet: Dropbox Dash uses a hybrid feature store to deliver fast, fresh signals at scale, keeping AI search accurate, low-latency, and reliable at scale. ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌
body_text:
----------
_Welcome to __**[Hello World](https://hw.glich.co/subscribe)**__, we help software engineers learn the art of building scalable and resilient systems._

_Last week’s concept: _[How Spotify Scaled Content Annotations to Millions (Without Losing Quality)](https://hw.glich.co/p/how-spotify-scaled-content-annotations-to-millions-without-losing-quality)


----------———————————————————————————

### Want to get the most out of ChatGPT?

View image: (https://media.beehiiv.com/cdn-cgi/image/fit=scale-down,format=auto,onerror=redirect,quality=80/uploads/asset/file/3d07933c-56f3-4e33-8801-be4127e06d2c/ChatGPT_V1.jpg?t=1744399039)
Follow image link: (https://offers.hubspot.com/using-chatgpt-at-work?utm_medium=email-media-newsletter&utm_source=6V9JLQD4WN&utm_campaign=creator&utm_content=beehiiv&utm_term=version-c&_bhiiv=opp_7c19cafd-6e80-4d05-99b6-fb39d61c90a9_b942af4d&bhcl_id=ce5a1c86-e857-4ac0-8b20-afb11be8e7d4_feacedb1-ede1-41f7-b75d-ece6c01a1158_a53bb2a6-08e1-4205-bc31-6f1ac38dd251)
Caption: 

ChatGPT is a superpower if you know how to use it correctly.

Discover how [HubSpot's guide to AI](https://offers.hubspot.com/using-chatgpt-at-work?utm_medium=email-media-newsletter&utm_source=6V9JLQD4WN&utm_campaign=creator&utm_content=beehiiv&utm_term=version-c&_bhiiv=opp_7c19cafd-6e80-4d05-99b6-fb39d61c90a9_b942af4d&bhcl_id=ce5a1c86-e857-4ac0-8b20-afb11be8e7d4_feacedb1-ede1-41f7-b75d-ece6c01a1158_a53bb2a6-08e1-4205-bc31-6f1ac38dd251) can elevate both your productivity and creativity to get more things done.

Learn to automate tasks, enhance decision-making, and foster innovation with the power of AI.

[Download the free guide](https://offers.hubspot.com/using-chatgpt-at-work?utm_medium=email-media-newsletter&utm_source=6V9JLQD4WN&utm_campaign=creator&utm_content=beehiiv&utm_term=version-c&_bhiiv=opp_7c19cafd-6e80-4d05-99b6-fb39d61c90a9_b942af4d&bhcl_id=ce5a1c86-e857-4ac0-8b20-afb11be8e7d4_feacedb1-ede1-41f7-b75d-ece6c01a1158_a53bb2a6-08e1-4205-bc31-6f1ac38dd251)

———————————————————————————

----------
### Job Opening

* Front End Entry Level @Albertsons Companies: 

[Front End Entry Level at Albertsons Companies](https://hw.glich.co/jobboard/jobs/front-end-entry-level-at-albertsons-companies-5m5ux9)

* Software Engineer - mobile / react native @Atlassian:
[Software Engineer - mobile / react native at Atlassian](https://hw.glich.co/jobboard/jobs/software-engineer-mobile-react-native-at-atlassian-ddftmn)

* Full Stack Java/React Developer @NTT DATA: 
[Full Stack Java/React Developer at NTT DATA](https://hw.glich.co/jobboard/jobs/full-stack-javareact-developer-at-ntt-data-xbf1co)

* Junior Software Engineer @Hexnode: 

[Junior Software Engineer at Hexnode](https://hw.glich.co/jobboard/jobs/junior-software-engineer-at-hexnode-p5nz1e)


----------———————————————————————————

When you ask an AI at work to “find the right document” or “show what my team is working on,” you expect the answer instantly and you expect it to be right. But behind that simple interaction is a complex system making thousands of decisions in real time.

Dropbox Dash is built to understand how people work across files, chats, and collaboration tools, and to surface what matters most in the moment. To do that, Dash relies on real-time machine learning that constantly adapts to user behaviour. At the center of this system is a “**Feature Store”**, a critical piece of infrastructure that delivers fresh, high-quality data signals to AI models in milliseconds. 

Let’s discover in this blog, how Dropbox built this feature store, why existing solutions fell short, and what it takes to power real-time AI at scale.

“If you haven’t gone through our first blog on Dropbox Dash, please check out: [How Dropbox Dash Uses Context Engineering to Build Smarter AI](https://hw.glich.co/p/how-dropbox-dash-uses-context-engineering-to-build-smarter-ai)“ — 

## Why Dash Needs a Feature Store

Dropbox Dash is designed to understand questions about your work including files, messages, shared docs, and collaboration patterns and surface the most relevant results instantly.

To do this, Dash doesn’t just look at filenames or keywords. It looks at **signals**, such as:

* What files you recently opened

* Who you collaborate with

* Which documents are trending in your team

* How content is being used right now

These signals are called **features**, and the system that stores, updates, and serves them to ML models is the **feature store**.

Because Dash evaluates **many documents per query**, it needs:

* Thousands of feature lookups per search

* Responses in under **100 milliseconds**

* Fresh data that reflects user behavior almost immediately

Off-the-shelf feature stores couldn’t meet these requirements.

## The Core Challenges Dropbox Faced

Building a feature store for Dash wasn’t straightforward. Dropbox had to deal with several constraints:

View image: (https://media.beehiiv.com/cdn-cgi/image/fit=scale-down,format=auto,onerror=redirect,quality=80/uploads/asset/file/8da83328-1d6e-4dbe-807c-9f662acab7ec/image.png?t=1769528923)
Caption: 

1. **Hybrid infrastructure: **Feature engineering happens in the cloud using Spark, while real-time serving runs on low-latency, on-prem systems. Most feature stores support only one environment.

2. **Massive parallel reads: **A single search query fans out into thousands of feature lookups across many files.

3. **Real-time freshness: **User actions like opening a document or joining a channel must affect results within minutes.

4. **Mixed computation styles: **Some features are batch-based (historical trends), others are real-time (recent activity). Both must work together.

## Designing a Hybrid Feature Store

When building the feature store for Dropbox Dash, the team didn’t want a rigid, off-the-shelf solution. They evaluated popular platforms like Feast, Hopsworks, and Tecton, and chose **Feast** as the found

subject: Pathiki Anantha, automate workflows and secure your business in 2026
snippet: Your March guide: Cybersecurity forecast, AI for lean teams, and 2026 roadmaps. Monthly Update | February 2026 Hi Pathiki Anantha, and welcome to our monthly update—designed to help your business punch
body_text:
=========================

Monthly Update | February 2026

=========================



Hi Pathiki Anantha, and welcome to our monthly update—designed to help your business punch above its weight.
 
For growing businesses, technology is the ultimate equaliser. This month, we focus on two critical pillars for 2026: Protection and Practicality. We’re diving into the cybersecurity forecast so you can anticipate threats before they happen, and breaking down AI into simple, actionable steps to automate your workflows.
 
Plus, you can get an insider look at the Google Cloud roadmap to ensure your IT investments are future-proof. Let's get started.


Spotlights


Cloud Technical Series: AI in Action*


Get hands-on to get ahead. Equip your team with the latest skills in Gen AI models and tooling. This two-day technical launchpad features classroom-style LiveLabs designed to accelerate your in-house capabilities.

Register now! <https://go.cloudplatformonline.com/ODA4LUdKVy0zMTQAAAGgCHX8QdKn8zq5xGnnAqf2u0LHfqfrHq9AozXPeVNwdsSX9xruosxTC-XnNeTMJDqIRWVCOIw=>


Cloud Roadmap Series: AI Innovation*


Plan your next move. Get a clear view of Google Cloud’s product strategy. Understand where AI models and agents are heading so you can align your technology roadmap with future innovation.
 
Save my spot! <https://go.cloudplatformonline.com/ODA4LUdKVy0zMTQAAAGgCHX8QddwcXlWBEQxwmo1SkV_cFf57R3M8bpx7rY98NGl5l2a50ox7s-H6TnZv8Qoxuwrl0s=>


Google Cloud Cybersecurity Forecast 2026


Anticipate the threats. Discover the cybersecurity trends most likely to impact SMBs in 2026 and learn how to stay secure as you scale.
 
Sign me up! <https://go.cloudplatformonline.com/ODA4LUdKVy0zMTQAAAGgCHX8Qbo7SCIlNn-nivvPEfb5t-PwzBmk5vpz80X3jfQgIz0pXSnawpY4LbG0N4X-tqetryw=>


Make AI Work for You: Core AI Concepts and Simple Steps for Workflow Automation

See this week's Educative Newsletter + new lessons in Grokking Modern System Design. ͏  ͏  ͏  ͏  ͏  ͏  ͏  ͏  ͏  ͏  ͏  ͏  ͏  ͏  ͏  ͏  ͏  ͏  ͏  ͏  ͏  ͏  ͏  ͏  ͏  ͏  ͏  ͏  ͏  ͏  ͏  ͏  ͏  ͏  ͏  ͏  ͏  ͏  ͏  ͏  ͏  ͏  ͏  ͏  ͏  ͏  ͏  ͏  ͏  ͏  ͏  ͏  ͏  ͏  ͏  ͏  ͏  ͏  ͏  ͏  ͏  ͏  ͏  ͏  ͏  ͏  ͏  ͏  ͏  ͏  ͏  ͏  ͏  ͏  ͏  ͏  ͏  ͏  ͏  ͏  ͏  ͏  ͏  ͏  ͏  ͏  ͏  ͏  ͏  ͏  ͏  ͏  ͏  ͏  ͏  ͏  ͏  ͏  ͏  ͏  ͏  ͏  ͏  ͏  ͏  ͏  ͏  ͏  ͏  ͏  ͏  ͏  ͏  ͏  ͏  ͏  ͏  ͏  ͏  ͏ ­ ­ ­ ­ ­ ­ ­ ­ ­ ­ ­ ­ ­ ­ ­ ­ ­ ­ ­ ­ ­ ­ ­ ­ ­ ­ ­ ­ ­ ­ ­ ­ ­ ­ ­ ­ ­ ­ ­ ­ ­ ­ ­ ­ ­ ­ ­ ­ ­ ­ ­ ­ ­ ­ ­ ­ ­ ­ ­ ­ ­ ­ ­ ­ ­ ­ ­ ­ ­ ­ ­ ­ ­ ­ ­ ­ ­ ­ ­ ­ ­ ­ ­ ­ ­ ­ ­ ­ ­ ­ ­ ­ ­ ­ ­ ­ ­ ­ ­ ­ ­ ­ ­ ­ ­ ­ ­ ­ ­ ­ ­ ­ ­ ­ ­ ­ ­ ­ ­ ­ ­ ­ ­ ­ ­ ­ ­ ­ ­ ­ ­ ­ ­ ­ ­ ­ ­ ­ ­ ­ ­ ­ ­ ­ ­ ­ ­ ­ ­ ­ ­ ­ ­ ­ ­ ­ ­ ­ ­ ­ ­ ­ ­ ­ ­ ­ ­ ­ ­ ­ ­ ­ ­ ­ ­ ­ ­ ­ ­ ­ ­ ­ ­ ­ ­ ­ ­ ­ ­ ­ ­ ­ ­ ­ ­

* Note: You are receiving this platform-related update because you have an active Educative account. If you wish to unsubscribe from product or other updates related to your account, you can do so anytime.

Ananth,

With your Educative account, you get free access to The Educative Newsletter. Here is this week's System Design edition:

-

Streaming intelligence enables instant, model-driven decisions (https://learn.educative.io/e3t/Ctc/RG+113/cz5H404/VW5-3175R5QWW42kTNT4vSm88W78L4Hk5KDftfN4Hzmf45m_5PW6N1X8z6lZ3mYVfmFXK3RJPgBW8jH9wZ4-dmhWW4PKzky8k8jNLW99hysP4q22rxVNKGNd91vPTgW224BJD6bPhBCW6lmywr2FRR8CW2RJbY53vLxvPN9b1801Lqp5YW42bVsP99TnbDW6wzfM32FbwJKW9lwbVj5wnrW2Vf6CTQ2fRSpgVwpFPQ5BSjq-W3WZ93B1X8YlsV45rgB86Dy8qW4SvrbF40GRWbW2_DQ8g6dRLcjW4Ky8KT82D5QyW9lNTcq870MCJW8sFv2d5GJw4TW3XKg7d502sgCN8dqqKk5qJ7tN2Yjw8YJ114QW7qWx9J6QmfMqW8jQlMV7fCXGlW1DdYc81RtTQFN7KMBM-QPVT3W7jPhX01SPybZW6B26k93Z0tztW7_HF9V5pnR-vW3fkgXJ6GRGh5W8k3M8S5mpr69VPVK4h6Lh0DPW865Y0B7lqBc0W62gY9-7hqQDkW6H_BlT5TqNrBW6fGnH-3lZSF2f3B2nfY04 )

In addition, we published a few updates to our popular course:

-

Grokking the Modern System Design Interview (https://learn.educative.io/e3t/Ctc/RG+113/cz5H404/VW5-3175R5QWW42kTNT4vSm88W78L4Hk5KDftfN4Hzmdb5m_5PW50kH_H6lZ3mwN2w2cc_CJH6wW6jtwy-2SskdgW5_C4Rd2Nf0CFW3H4DPP8ts34nW9bmvcM4MCWgnW8SmWTr26WFc1W4-zf664Z-hhQW5kpnBJ3SFNjtW1WVG348L_8R3W3ZR0kg2NdwhGMVmgb1Rwh8cW8g5Nh17Q2YvkW4M8cDG8llh9MW6CJ66Y2T59dNN49KYj-l0z8HW403nxg4Y4GccW9bSl5b64X5nKW3WXjQn7ldXqSW5-VJG38hkm4lW3bvklr2Fvy1GW19B1xJ3FVrwNW7GyXLh6-mzcDW2rCyGf3McGdcW1_jj_w5M3DBJW96Vgf77DfLwLW1b1__c4FjTWRW5SLr7w8c2mVfVR1HWJ7ZB-2HW96_kmr6y_K4RW8Y5WpZ3B7-6zW7Z8B015rcbBZW19dcGP995HNddZgBj404 )

New lessons cover event-driven architectures, stream processing patterns, and designing low-latency data pipelines

Please enjoy.

– The Educative Team

body_text:
Is your roadmap AI-ready? We've revamped our Frontend, Backend, and MLOps paths and are adding Claude Code to the catalog.

View in browser (https://updates.roadmap.sh/e3t/Ctc/L2+113/d5qLSh04/VW3YYn39fS8wW9bgQnJ7KwZJFW7zr5cm5KDJvLKqQH5m_5PW7lCGcx6lZ3m7W3TgVkZ36VJHFW4hfzzj82BRynW2TK9jm5bsrdqW3H-4fG7qH5WlW7PCMwR7C_HVfW7LkP4p5j4Q6lN1w3htDXWwPbMVhqRC-R8gBW3zzQnL20FjpkW5QL7RH4QC6k4N6_RHm_8qmYSW6__PRv3pDxtFW1Y6TgD2ZPn8GW9d1czh6m36tsW4BNrSY3SH8ggW3ltKz52GDlGVW20RvhL5Rs6g9W5kBb5v2-hrQHN3SWQT6fL6sDW59xnB614KjD0Vhyjx27SgSg4W6d1Gz72Gy73lW8XbT9G7VXqtFW8cc-GF7BqMDVW64FSzy3NYWrDW17_p3P7HykkBN5BvWzf1DZGnW5fcrg55sHc5CW6thHkb4r47wtW3Th0vH3gZ7nhW4QrmPr96HdJdW5tV3R77pV-fmW5Z_VMn3jG_5FW320M-c6qzSvCW1QZyx73HMx8DW5yKGwh6GLvc8N7-gXmhjY5K3W3LCV711Sl9YDW84dXgP5cctvpW2r45DP740n9Df3RGwbW04 )

roadmap.sh (https://updates.roadmap.sh/e3t/Ctc/L2+113/d5qLSh04/VW3YYn39fS8wW9bgQnJ7KwZJFW7zr5cm5KDJvLKqQ43gP0xW69sMD-6lZ3m2W1QsXP63vB6hCW44DZZY6_cPtNVG8Bx21R4xkYW2DTVRF8pGz11W54xnkB6fYD45W4H0z5H2_1BfcW8mClfP2B_10kW4c5MkM1swKhSVmfgrv2kPtwnW8r8pGf33tLm2W4kDy2g8nfvxBN1lQQjbwYtcGW1pnFD24g3V8FW1JvQ955lqVR1W5zlhbN5k2KNgW3g6BFj4RzWt_V-1nvP2X8CPDW1_DLGD269LWxW1mKS713-SbwfW99SxL55b7L8kf1_m5dd04 )

Moderne_Logo (https://updates.roadmap.sh/e3t/Ctc/L2+113/d5qLSh04/VW3YYn39fS8wW9bgQnJ7KwZJFW7zr5cm5KDJvLKqQH3prCCW7lCdLW6lZ3ldW7lF0xm2ZMNfGW7Y7Wr270Vj7XW42m4rm3BQt62W9fXj2q64TX9HW4NmSnT62YW34W7sxB3X6hNB0TW3zKbCk1mMDWNVFr6fQ5PmvVGW7_k_CQ8kG4RpW2rdsr52DWzPfW7CqRwH4qNdtSW1hF-mV57krG-Vts0n911cWkwW9jM6pM4gPvwqN8lYgMh31pfzW2VjQ7j8K9cfFW8x6XR64mYchfW38fVsd1JnkGsW4C9RLb1YR9FMW6njtqN9fqzL_W6Qs-3M1XLf5RVdq-y29jS-_rM43WM3kN4fsW8Mfm-F9d4x6sf9gL2yY04 )

Hello Ananth,

It’s Kamran from roadmap.sh. The tech landscape is shifting faster than ever, and we're evolving with it. Today, we're excited to announce that we’ve published two new, highly requested roadmaps and continued our catalog review with a series of revamps to help you integrate AI in your daily workflow. We also have an exciting announcement from our sister brand, Towards Data Science. Read on to learn more!

NewRoadmap_16x9_AvailableNow (5) (https://updates.roadmap.sh/e3t/Ctc/L2+113/d5qLSh04/VW3YYn39fS8wW9bgQnJ7KwZJFW7zr5cm5KDJvLKqQ43gP0xW69sMD-6lZ3pWW1v3P0t4QmNY_W6V8-Qt5xbSqlW628SyV6DYx99W20kY9j2ZmQq6V1BG4h5VXwM8W8FmHG_2sGpLYW4Kmgh93jN3TxW99FTVX3LWqZ2W8F_Mm_3QPjyYMjbRC8HS_gJW1sv78P7YyqRKVpQQZc4chjXLN6j0lNy3qZJ4W8KsRfZ19x5JwW4FGvJ64ytTBFN7vltH-CWhbRW4N234X65RN5LW5R2XWq3DSZG3W12LxTt3FcbhpN5GJYXF_q53QdxgbDl04 )

Ruby Roadmap

Ruby is an elegant, expressive, and powerful scripting language designed for backend web development. It's a robust, dynamically typed language, widely famous for its simplicity. We’ve covered everything you need to know to get started in Ruby (https://updates.roadmap.sh/e3t/Ctc/L2+113/d5qLSh04/VW3YYn39fS8wW9bgQnJ7KwZJFW7zr5cm5KDJvLKqQ43gP0xW69sMD-6lZ3pWW1v3P0t4QmNY_W6V8-Qt5xbSqlW628SyV6DYx99W20kY9j2ZmQq6V1BG4h5VXwM8W8FmHG_2sGpLYW4Kmgh93jN3TxW99FTVX3LWqZ2W8F_Mm_3QPjyYMjbRC8HS_gJW1sv78P7YyqRKVpQQZc4chjXLN6j0lNy3qZJ4W8KsRfZ19x5JwW4FGvJ64ytTBFN7vltH-CWhbRW4N234X65RN5LW5R2XWq3DSZG3W12LxTt3FcbhpN5GJYXF_q53QdxgbDl04 ) programming.

Check out the Ruby Roadmap
(https://updates.roadmap.sh/e3t/Ctc/L2+113/d5qLSh04/VW3YYn39fS8wW9bgQnJ7KwZJFW7zr5cm5KDJvLKqQ43gP0xW69sMD-6lZ3pWW1v3P0t4QmNY_W6V8-Qt5xbSqlW628SyV6DYx99W20kY9j2ZmQq6V1BG4h5VXwM8W8FmHG_2sGpLYW4Kmgh93jN3TxW99FTVX3LWqZ2W8F_Mm_3QPjyYMjbRC8HS_gJW1sv78P7YyqRKVpQQZc4chjXLN6j0lNy3qZJ4W8KsRfZ19x5JwW4FGvJ64ytTBFN7vltH-CWhbRW4N234X65RN5LW5R2XWq3DSZG3W12LxTt3FcbhpN5GJYXF_q53QdxgbDl04 )

Ruby on Rails (https://updates.roadmap.sh/e3t/Ctc/L2+113/d5qLSh04/VW3YYn39fS8wW9bgQnJ7KwZJFW7zr5cm5KDJvLKqQn3gP0xW6N1vHY6lZ3lqV9wjGl4nxg8yVN8WVs6Vrjc3N8l16-3Ns0LPW2dVyyc1MCXTCVT2sgK1n5jjfW5Bdkwx3nn_D0N2p89kb-NR1JW4mlVTT2Gk8w5N2M63gCZzZRjW6FDC5F7lfKr0W3FJf932L21LBW4JyNwG2jh8WqW4pMkMm11_zS9W34L9sb4mF5FSW6b7CFm4vrXPFW3PBFYB1dx2BSW8lkCq373Z8Z6N4Xt4C5jfMKmW6HTRZB77B3vVW3xgDn450ktz2W1N6gXh1M3_6HW4FJb_l3SrmGnf3jkV9604 )

Ruby on Rails Roadmap

Ruby on Rails is the perfect tool for Ruby developers to create robust and powerful apps. With this new roadmap (https://updates.roadmap.sh/e3t/Ctc/L2+113/d5qLSh04/VW3YYn39fS8wW9bgQnJ7KwZJFW7zr5cm5KDJvLKqQn3gP0xW6N1vHY6lZ3lqV9wjGl4nxg8yVN8WVs6Vrjc3N8l16-3Ns0LPW2dVyyc1MCXTCVT2sgK1n5jjfW5Bdkwx3nn_D0N2p89kb-NR1JW4mlVTT2Gk8w5N2M63gCZzZRjW6FDC5F7lfKr0W3FJf932L21LBW4JyNwG2jh8WqW4pMkMm11_zS9W34L9sb4mF5FSW6b7CFm4vrXPFW3PBFYB1dx2BSW8lkCq373Z8Z6N4Xt4C5jfMKmW6HTRZB77B3vVW3xgDn450ktz2W1N6gXh1M3_6HW4FJb_l3SrmGnf3jkV9604 ) , we cover the foundations you need to build, deploy, and maintain production-ready web applications.

Visit the Ruby on Rails Roadmap
(https://updates.roadmap.sh/e3t/Ctc/L2+113/d5qLSh04/VW3YYn39fS8wW9bgQnJ7KwZJFW7zr5cm5KDJvLKqQn3gP0xW6N1vHY6lZ3lqV9wjGl4nxg8yVN8WVs6Vrjc3N8l16-3Ns0LPW2dVyyc1MCXTCVT2sgK1n5jjfW5Bdkwx3nn_D0N2p89kb-NR1JW4mlVTT2Gk8w5N2M63gCZzZRjW6FDC5F7lfKr0W3FJf932L21LBW4JyNwG2jh8WqW4pMkMm11_zS9W34L9sb4mF5FSW6b7CFm4vrXPFW3PBFYB1dx2BSW8lkCq373Z8Z6N4Xt4C5jfMKmW6HTRZB77B3vVW3xgDn450ktz2W1N6gXh1M3_6HW4FJb_l3SrmGnf3jkV9604 )

Together with Moderne

Code Remix Summit | May 11-13, Miami

If modernization only lives on a roadmap, nothing changes. Code Remix Summit is for engineering leaders ready to fix legacy fast. Come away with practical, applied approaches to modernizing real production systems.

subject: 1,200 Applicants in 12 Hours. Are You One of Them?
snippet: Hi, ₹12–20 LPA SDE roles are increasingly becoming the new normal for well prepared developers Microsoft posted an SDE role at 7 PM yesterday. By 7 AM today, there were 1200+ applicants. In a crowd of
body_text:
Hi,

₹12–20 LPA SDE roles are increasingly becoming the new normal for well prepared developers

Microsoft posted an SDE role at 7 PM yesterday. By 7 AM today, there were 1200+ applicants.

In a crowd of 1,200 applicants, differentiation is no longer optional.

Because when 1,200 people apply for the same role, effort alone doesn’t make you stand out.
 

Strategy does.

Soluti

subject: Adapting to what’s next in work and leadership
snippet: Artificial intelligence is no longer a future trend, it is actively reshaping… ͏ ͏ ͏ ͏ ͏ ͏ ͏ ͏ ͏ ͏ ͏ ͏ ͏ ͏ ͏ ͏ ͏ ͏ ͏ ͏ ͏ ͏ ͏ ͏ ͏ ͏ ͏ ͏ ͏ ͏ ͏ ͏ ͏ ͏ ͏ ͏ ͏ ͏ ͏ ͏ ͏ ͏ ͏ ͏ ͏ ͏ ͏ ͏ ͏ ͏ ͏ ͏ ͏ ͏ ͏ ͏ ͏ ͏ ͏ ͏ ͏
body_text:
In the Loop

By LinkedIn


By LinkedIn

    
    
    Read this article on LinkedIn to join the conversation
Read on LinkedIn
https://www.linkedin.com/comm/pulse/adapting-whats-next-work-leadership-linkedin-ksrge?lipi=urn%3Ali%3Apage%3Aemail_email_series_follow_newsletter_01%3BF%2FmpBcIiQNqWkby3xI7P1g%3D%3D&midToken=AQGQJvLLYDDPBA&midSig=1-BFpwzy4aus81&trk=eml-email_series_follow_newsletter_01-null-0-read_more_banner_cta_&trkEmail=eml-email_series_follow_newsletter_01-null-0-read_more_banner_cta_-null-g1ev5b~mlqktet4~t6-null-null&eid=g1ev5b-mlqktet4-t6&otpToken=MWIwMzE3ZTgxMTJmYzBjY2I3MjQwNGVkNDExZmU0Yjc4ZWNmZDY0MTkxYWU4NzYxNzljNDAyNjY0YzVhNWRmNWZlZGNkZmJmMTNiMWUzZDc2NDhmZWIxNjdhNzA2MmYzMjBjOTMyMTc0N2NkOWVhYmQxZTQxMSwxLDE%3D
  
Artificial intelligence is no longer a future trend, it is actively reshaping how we work, lead and build careers. This week, we’re exploring what it takes to stay resilient in an AI-powered economy: from a new book offering a practical framework for adapting your skills, to a candid policy conversation with one of Congress’ leading AI voices, to a real-world playbook for making your next career move. If you’re thinking about what comes next, this edition is for you. Pre-order Open to Work: A playbook for thriving in the AI era As AI reshapes the workplace, the question isn’t whether your role will change, it’s how you’ll adapt. That’s the focus of Open to Work, LinkedIn’s first book, Co-authored by Ryan Roslansky and Aneesh Raman, the book draws on insights from LinkedIn and Microsoft, to explain what is truly changing and what skills matter most in an AI-powered economy. The book introduces the Three Bucket Framework, a practical way to think about what to prioritize at work, what to future-proof and what AI can take off your plate. If you’re navigating today’s job market, exploring how to integrate AI into your work or simply thinking about what comes next, this book is designed to help. Pre-order Open to Work here.  The Messy Middle tackles AI, careers and the future of work The premiere episode of The Messy Middle is now live. In its debut, the podcast features Jay Obernolte — widely regarded as one of Congress’ leading voices on artificial intelligence — in a wide-ranging conversation about leadership, lifelong learning and the future of work. Obernolte’s path to Capitol Hill has been anything but conventional: from advanced study in AI to video game design, small-business ownership, aviation and eventually public service. That nonlinear journey informs his perspective on how the U.S. approaches AI regulation and what professionals should prioritize as technology reshapes careers. The episode explores: Why intellectual curiosity may be the most valuable workplace skill What video game design and entrepreneurship reveal about adaptability The prediction that many professionals will have three distinct careers over a lifetime How to stay relevant and resilient during rapid technical change The Messy Middle is a monthly podcast focused on complex public policy issues at the intersection of tech, law and the world of work, offering a candid look at the people shaping these debates. The premiere is now live—watch, listen, and subscribe. 🎧 Spotify: https://lnkd.in/etG7HpZj 🎧 Apple: https://lnkd.in/eNJp6ZPp Robin Arzón on making your next career move Missed it the first time or ready to take notes? Now’s your chance to watch the replay of How to Train for Your Next Career Move featuring Robin Arzón of Peloton Interactive. In this candid conversation, Arzón shares how she pivoted from corporate litigator to vice president of fitness programming and head instructor at Peloton  and the clear principles that helped her make a bold, strategic transition. This is a live replay of a previously recorded event, featuring real-time commentary and community discussion. Click “Attend” to watch the replay and add your questions to the chat: https://lnkd.in/gZHsCd5j The conversation is exclusive to Premium subscribers. Not yet a subscriber? Discover how LinkedIn Premium can help you reach your goals faster: https://lnkd.in/djNtgBdr 💡 Subscribe for more career tips, tools and trends from across LinkedIn In the Loop.
Keep reading on LinkedIn
https://www.linkedin.com/comm/pulse/adapting-whats-next-work-leadership-linkedin-ksrge?lipi=urn%3Ali%3Apage%3Aemail_email_series_follow_newsletter_01%3BF%2FmpBcIiQNqWkby3xI7P1g%3D%3D&midToken=AQGQJvLLYDDPBA&midSig=1-BFpwzy4aus81&trk=eml-email_series_follow_newsletter_01-newsletter_content_preview_text-0-readmore_button_&trkEmail=eml-email_series_follow_newsletter_01-newsletter_content_preview_text-0-readmore_button_-null-g1ev5b~mlqktet4~t6-null-null&eid=g1ev5b-mlqktet4-t6&otpToken=MWIwMzE3ZTgxMTJmYzBjY2I3MjQwNGVkNDExZmU0Yjc4ZWNmZDY0MTkxYWU4NzYxNzljNDAyNjY0YzVhNWRmNWZlZGNkZmJmMTNiMWUzZDc2NDhmZWIxNjdhNzA2MmYzMjBjOTMyMTc0N2NkOWVhYmQxZTQxMSwxLDE%3D
  
      

----------------------------------------

This email was intended for Anantha Kashyap Pathiki (AI/ML Graduate | Python, Django, SQL | Backend Development & ML Projects)

subject: Experience a powerful keynote, learning, networking, and more at GTC
snippet: Get front-row insights at the keynote, roll up your sleeves in expert-led labs, and see the latest AI discoveries in action. ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌
body_text:
Get front-row insights at the keynote, roll up your sleeves in expert-led labs, and see the latest AI discoveries in action. Brilliant Minds, Breakthrough Discoveries Register Now Join us at GTC 2026 to catch NVIDIA CEO Jensen Huang’s must-see keynote live at SAP Center on Monday, March 16, at 11 a.m. PT. Be sure to get there early to watch the NVIDIA GTC Live pregame starting at 8 a.m. PT. After the keynote, head over to the GTC campus and get hands-on with the latest AI technologies and meet with like minds. Go deep into the event experiences, from hands-on demos in the expo hall to gatherings in GTC Park and throughout downtown for training, hackathons, and specialty foods to power your brain. But before then, dive into the GTC session catalog spanning agentic and reasoning AI, open models, CUDA and developer tools, and more to discover how AI can be productive for the work you do. Here are some recommendations to get you started.
  • CUDA: New Features and Beyond
  • Python All the Way Down: Speed-of-Light CUDA Without Leaving Python
  • Maximize Memory Bandwidth on Modern GPUs: Practical Techniques, Patterns, and Worked Examples
  • Discover Top In-Demand Learning Paths Across Europe, the Middle East, and Africa
  • Decoding Quantum Errors With AI
Register Now NVIDIA Training and Certification Elevate your skills onsite at GTC with nine full-day workshops and 70+ immersive two-hour training labs. Learn about cutting-edge topics like AI infrastructure, generative AI, data science, robotics, graphics, and high-performance computing. You can even earn an NVIDIA technical certification for free—up to a $400 value. Explore Training Thank You to Our Sponsors Elite Diamond Privacy Center | Manage Pref

subject: Replication vs Redundancy. What's the Difference?
snippet: Learn the key differences between replication and redundancy to optimize your data protection strategies. Discover which method suits your needs best. ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌
body_text:
----------
_Welcome to __**[Hello World](https://hw.glich.co/subscribe)**__, we help software engineers learn the art of building scalable and resilient systems._

_Last week’s concept: _**[_How Dropbox Dash Uses a Feature Store for Real-time AI._](https://hw.glich.co/p/how-dropbox-dash-uses-a-feature-store-for-real-time-ai)**


----------———————————————————————————

----------
## Anyshift - Your new AI on-call engineer.

View image: (https://media.beehiiv.com/cdn-cgi/image/fit=scale-down,format=auto,onerror=redirect,quality=80/uploads/asset/file/a4d410e9-3cff-4e5a-ba87-ad5012a1e4f0/Anyshift_Hello_world.jpeg?t=1771354503)
Follow image link: (https://www.anyshift.io/homepage?utm_source=hello-world&utm_medium=newsletter&utm_campaign=creator-q1-2026&utm_content=replace_your_on-call_engineer)
Caption: 

AI on-call engineer for SRE teams. It connects your entire stack, maps every dependency, and finds what broke, cutting your MTTR in half.

[**See it in action**](https://www.anyshift.io/homepage?utm_source=hello-world&utm_medium=newsletter&utm_campaign=creator-q1-2026&utm_content=replace_your_on-call_engineer)


----------———————————————————————————

### Job Opening

* Software Engineer - @Nexthink:

[Software Engineer - @Nexthink](https://hw.glich.co/jobboard/jobs/software-engineer-at-nexthink-n4g66z)

* Senior Cloud Deployment - @KPMG:
[Senior Cloud Deployment - @KPMG](https://hw.glich.co/jobboard/jobs/senior-cloud-development-at-kpmg-india-nimfq3)

* Java Developer @Conduent:
[Java Developer @Conduent](https://hw.glich.co/jobboard/jobs/java-developer-at-conduent-qghm7r)

* Software Engineer 1 @Booking Holdings:

[Software Engineer 1 @Booking Holdings](https://hw.glich.co/jobboard/jobs/software-engineer-i-at-booking-holdings-mybidr)

———————————————————————————

Think of it this way: **replication uses **_**active**_** copies** of data or components. They’re live, online, and handling traffic right alongside the primary. In stark contrast, **redundancy uses **_**passive**_** copies** that just sit there, idle, waiting for the moment the main component fails so they can jump in.

### **Defining Replication and Redundancy**

While both strategies are designed to make a system more reliable, they get there in completely different ways. Replication is all about being proactive it distributes the workload to boost performance and ensure the service never skips a beat. Redundancy is purely reactive, acting as a safety net for when things inevitably go wrong.

Getting this distinction right is the first step toward designing a system that’s not just resilient, but also efficient.

View image: (https://media.beehiiv.com/cdn-cgi/image/fit=scale-down,format=auto,onerror=redirect,quality=80/uploads/asset/file/9b470409-d2a2-49b5-a419-573f7abf7720/7b5911df-f733-4476-9528-dc60d897f1ab.jpg?t=1771185106)
Caption: 

To make the comparison even clearer, let's break down the fundamental differences side-by-side.

### **Replication vs Redundancy at a Glance**

**Criterion**


**Replication**


**Redundancy**



**Primary Goal**


High availability and performance


Fault tolerance and disaster recovery



**State of Copies**


Active (all copies handle live operations)


Passive (backup copies are idle until needed)



**Resource Usage**


High (all components are operational)


Low (standby components consume minimal resources)



**Use Case Example**


A distributed database with multiple read replicas


A data center with a backup power generator




In short, replication keeps multiple components running simultaneously to share the load and prevent downtime. Redundancy keeps a spare on the sidelines, ready to be tagged in only when a failure occurs.

## **Understanding Redundancy as a Safety Net**

Think of redundancy as your system's insurance policy. It's a safety net designed to catch you when a component fails. Unlike replication, which keeps multiple active copies running, redundancy is all about **passive, standby resources** that sit idle during normal operations. Their one job? To jump into action the moment a primary system goes offline, keeping everything running.

View image: (https://media.beehiiv.com/cdn-cgi/image/fit=scale-down,format=auto,onerror=redirect,quality=80/uploads/asset/file/ae4b563c-0e17-479c-9167-c60e04447e7d/6ec4b337-c979-4621-badf-d5e4f00a0e47.jpg?t=1771185107)
Caption: 

This approach is the bedrock of fault tolerance. It's how you prevent a single point of failure from taking down your entire application. While people often mix up the two terms, their origins tell a different story. Redundancy originated in the mainframe era, purely as a hardware strategy to handle faults.

“_Redundancy focuses on having duplicate, passive components ready to activate upon failure. This strategy is reactive, designed to minimize downtime rather than enhance performance._“ — 

### **Practical Examples of Redundancy**

In the real world, redundancy is all about reliability, not speed. A classic example is the **N+1 configuration** in data centers. For every 'N' essential components, like power supplies, they keep one extra (+1) on standby. This simple but effective strategy helps enterprises achieve **99.99% availability**, which works out to just over 52 minutes of downtime a year. You can get a deeper look at system reliability in this [_**detailed article from GeeksforGeeks**_](https://www.geeksforgeeks.org/system-design/difference-between-redundancy-and-replication/).

A much simpler example is the backup internet connection in an office. It does absolutely nothing until the main line drops. A backup database server that only gets updated periodically is another perfect illustration of redundancy in action. It’s worth noting that resilience often involves multiple layers, and you can see how [_**caching strategies like Redis and Memcached**_](https://hw.glich.co/p/redis
